meta.schema_ref: plugin:UnrealAiEditor/Resources/UnrealAiToolCatalog.json (single embedded catalog, no separate schema file)

status_legend.implemented: Dispatcher has a UE handler in UnrealAiToolDispatch*.cpp; calls may still fail with invalid args or engine state.
status_legend.future: Staged capability in catalog; implementation may be partial — verify in source.
status_legend.designed: Contract drafted; behavior may be incomplete.
status_legend.deprecated: Prefer the replacement tool_id; see docs/tooling/tool-dispatch-inventory.md.

tool_surface.description: Optional per-tool overrides under tools[].tool_surface — domain_tags, centrality, worst_case_cost, requires_strict_args (tools-expansion.md). Tiered eligibility, usage prior, JSONL logging, and repair nudges are toggled in source: Plugins/UnrealAiEditor/.../Misc/UnrealAiRuntimeDefaults.h (not environment variables).

invocation.focused_flag: Optional JSON field "focused": true on supported tools. Stripped before handlers; after a successful run the editor opens or foregrounds the related Blueprint, asset, Content Browser selection, or project source file in the IDE. Tools list this property explicitly where the catalog schema is strict (additionalProperties: false).

tools.summary: Attach child actor to parent.

child_path.description: World path of the child actor (same as child_actor_path).

parent_path.description: World path of the parent actor (same as parent_actor_path).

actor_path.description: When paired with parent_path or parent_actor_path, treated as the child actor path.

tools.summary: Destroy an actor in the editor world. Refused for engine streaming/HLOD/WorldPartition infrastructure actors (class/path heuristics)—only delete user-authored props/meshes you can justify.

confirm.description: Assent to delete: DELETE, yes, y, true, confirm, or 1 (case-insensitive).

failure_modes: ok:false when actor_path matches built-in protected types (HLOD / WorldPartition helpers): choose a different actor or fix the user's mesh instead.

tools.summary: Find actors by outliner label, internal actor name, full object path substring, or exact actor_path match.
Read actor transform and mobility.

Mutate/set actor transform in the loaded level. Use this after scene_fuzzy_search when you need a concrete position/rotation/scale change; do not use for discovery or status-only checks (use scene_fuzzy_search or actor_get_transform). Omit `transform` and top-level location/rotation/scale to keep the current pose (useful with `teleport_physics` only). Use `transform` object, or top-level `location`/`rotation`/`scale` number arrays [x,y,z]. `actor_path` accepts full path or a unique suffix (e.g. PersistentLevel.Actor_UAID_...).

transform.description: Optional if using top-level location[]/rotation[]/scale[] instead. Arrays inside transform also accepted ([x,y,z] form).

location.description: Optional when transform omitted: [x,y,z] applied with current rotation/scale.

rotation.description: Optional when transform omitted: [pitch,yaw,roll] with current location/scale.

scale.description: Optional when transform omitted: [x,y,z] scale with current location/rotation.

tools.summary: Read whether an actor is currently hidden in the editor.
Toggle actor visibility.
Toggle whether the actor is temporarily hidden in the editor (flips current state). Prefer actor_set_visibility when you need an explicit hidden true/false.

Spawn an actor in the editor world. Requires class_path + location[3] + rotation[3]. If exact transform is unknown, use explicit safe defaults (e.g. location [0,0,100], rotation [0,0,0]) instead of omitting fields.

class_path.description: Must be a subclass of AActor (e.g. /Game/MyActor.MyActor_C or /Script/Engine.StaticMeshActor). Components (USceneComponent, etc.) cannot be spawned as actors.

failure_modes: Missing required args: class_path, location, and rotation are mandatory; `{}` is invalid.

tools.summary: Summarize AnimBlueprint graphs.

focused.description: After success, open this AnimBlueprint in the editor.

tools.summary: Delete assets.
Duplicate asset to new path.
Read asset metadata and dependencies summary.
Import files via automated import pipeline.
Open an asset in its native editor when you already have an exact object path (for example from asset_index_fuzzy_search or asset_registry_query). Use for inspection/focus, not discovery.

object_path.description: Canonical asset object path. Preferred key. Example: /Game/Blueprints/MyBP.MyBP.

focused.description: After success, foreground the asset editor (redundant with tool behavior if already opening).

tools.summary: Query Asset Registry for deterministic listing by exact path/class filters. You must pass at least path_filter (or class_name)—unbounded full-registry scans are rejected. `{}` is invalid. Use asset_index_fuzzy_search for fuzzy name discovery under a path_prefix.

path_filter.description: Package path prefix (for example /Game or /Game/Blueprints). Preferred key.

object_path.description: Compatibility alias: object path converted to package path for path_filter.

class_name.description: Single class path filter (for example /Script/Engine.Blueprint). Preferred key.

class_paths.description: Compatibility alias for class_name; first entry is used.

class_filters.description: Compatibility alias for class_name; first entry is used.

class_names.description: Compatibility alias for class_name; first entry is used. Short names are expanded to /Script/Engine.<Name>.

filters.description: Compatibility alias for structured callers. Primary shape is array entries like {"key":"class","value":"/Script/Engine.Blueprint"} and {"key":"path","value":"/Game"}; object shape with path_prefix/path_filter is also tolerated at runtime.

failure_modes: Neither path_filter nor class_name: provide a bounded filter (handler rejects empty filters).

tools.summary: Rename/move asset and optionally fix references.

Persist dirty package changes to disk. Use after compile/edit/mutation passes so editor state matches files on disk; do not use for discovery or read-only status checks. When all_dirty is true, saves all dirty packages (no explicit paths required).
Preview sound in editor.
Add member variable to Blueprint.

type.description: Variable type token (preferred key). Alias: variable_type.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint in the editor.

tools.summary: Compile a Blueprint and return diagnostics. Use this after blueprint_apply_ir or graph edits to validate/fix compile state; do not use as a graph discovery tool (use blueprint_get_graph_summary or blueprint_export_ir for read-only inspection). Use format_graphs to run in-process graph layout on script graphs before compile when graphs should be laid out.

format_graphs.description: When true, run bundled graph layout on all non-empty ubergraph, function, and macro graphs before compiling.

focused.description: After success, open and foreground this Blueprint in the editor.

tools.summary: Apply compact Blueprint IR and materialize/wire EventGraph nodes. Primary tool for gameplay logic (overlap, timers, variables, calls). blueprint_path must be an object_path from asset_index_fuzzy_search or asset_registry_query results—never a guessed name when multiple assets matched. Use create_if_missing + parent_class for new BPs under /Game; pair with blueprint_compile. Minimal valid shape: blueprint_path + nodes[] + links[] where each node has node_id/op and each link has from/to pin refs. Optional layout_mode (single_row|multi_strand) and wire_knots (off|light|aggressive) override project defaults when auto_layout runs. graph_comment nodes support name, width, height, member_node_ids for comment boxes. blueprint_path must be a Blueprint asset path (/Game/...Asset.Asset), never a level actor/world path. Not for UMG Designer layout.

blueprint_path.description: Blueprint asset path under /Game. Accepted forms: /Game/Folder/MyBP or /Game/Folder/MyBP.MyBP.

create_if_missing.description: When true and blueprint_path is not on disk yet, create an Actor-based Blueprint at that path before applying IR (must be under /Game).

parent_class.description: Optional native class path when using create_if_missing (default /Script/Engine.Actor).

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint and focus graph_name when set.

merge_policy.description: How to combine with existing graphs. append_to_existing: reuse the first matching ReceiveBeginPlay/ReceiveTick (etc.) in the target graph and wire new exec from the exec tail (warns on duplicate anchors). create_new: always spawn IR nodes. Default: append_to_existing on ubergraph EventGraph, create_new on function/macro graphs.

layout_scope.description: When auto_layout is true: ir_nodes runs formatter on IR materialized nodes only; full_graph runs LayoutEntireGraph on the whole target graph. Default ir_nodes.

auto_layout.description: When true (default), run bundled graph layout after wiring when every IR node has x,y at 0. formatter_available is normally true in editor builds.

layout_mode.description: Optional layout strategy when auto_layout runs. Omit to use Unreal AI Editor project default.

wire_knots.description: Optional data-wire reroute knot pass. Omit to use project default.

nodes.description: IR nodes to materialize. Minimal node object shape: {"node_id":"id","op":"event_begin_play"}.

node_id.description: Stable node reference used by links/defaults.

width.description: Optional; graph_comment box width in graph units.

height.description: Optional; graph_comment box height in graph units.

member_node_ids.description: For graph_comment: node_id strings to geometrically refit the comment around after layout.

links.description: Connections using pin refs in node_id.pin format.

from.description: Source pin reference in node_id.pin form (example: beginplay.Then).

to.description: Target pin reference in node_id.pin form (example: delay.execute).

failure_modes: invalid_ir
asset_not_found
create_failed
graph_not_found
apply_failed
Bad blueprint_path: must be /Game/... Blueprint object path; resolve with asset_index_fuzzy_search before writes.
New asset: use create_if_missing:true or asset_create, then apply IR.

tools.summary: Run in-process layout on a Blueprint script graph: full graph or current selection, optional single-row vs multi-strand exec layout, data-wire knots, and comment reflow. Default format_scope is full_graph.

graph_name.description: Optional; default is the first ubergraph page.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint and focus the graph when set.

format_scope.description: full_graph: LayoutEntireGraph. selection: layout currently selected nodes in the open graph editor.

layout_mode.description: Optional; omit for Unreal AI Editor project default.

wire_knots.description: Optional data-wire knot pass; omit for project default.

tools.summary: Same layout pipeline as blueprint_format_graph but always targets the editor’s current graph selection (Blueprint graph tab must be open with nodes selected). Optional layout_mode and wire_knots override project defaults.

graph_name.description: Optional; defaults to the focused ubergraph page when omitted.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint and focus the graph when set.

failure_modes: no_selection
graph_tab_not_open

tools.summary: Read-only: bounded summary of Blueprint graph(s) for an existing asset. `blueprint_path` is required and must be a concrete /Game Blueprint object_path (prefer reuse from asset_index_fuzzy_search/asset_registry_query results; do not guess names). Optional graph_name narrows to one graph; omit to summarize ubergraph + functions + macros.

blueprint_path.description: Blueprint asset object path under /Game (aliases: object_path, asset_path). Not a level actor path.

graph_name.description: Optional; when set, only that graph is summarized if it exists.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint (and graph when named).

failure_modes: Load failure: verify path with asset_index_fuzzy_search; create asset first if missing.
Wrong path kind: PersistentLevel actor paths are not Blueprint assets—use /Game/... object paths.

tools.summary: Open Blueprint editor focused on a graph.
Line/sphere trace in editor world (collision, raycast, hit from camera or points).

Run a bounded editor console exec via allow-list keys (default). command must be a key: stat_fps, stat_unit, stat_gpu (no args); r_vsync requires args "0" or "1"; viewmode_lit | viewmode_unlit | viewmode_wireframe (no args). Unsafe legacy: pass raw console text only if Editor Settings → Console command: legacy wide exec, or set UNREAL_AI_CONSOLE_COMMAND_LEGACY_EXEC=1.

command.description: Allow-list key (e.g. stat_fps, r_vsync). Legacy wide-exec mode: full console line.

args.description: Optional. For r_vsync only: "0" or "1". For legacy wide-exec: appended after command with a space.

failure_modes: Unknown command key (default mode)
r_vsync without args 0/1
Legacy mode: substring policy blocks quit/exit/shader rebuild/crash

tools.summary: Navigate Content Browser to a folder.

Focus the Content Browser on a registered asset by object path (`path` / `object_path`). Resolves via the asset registry—use a full `/Game/.../Name.Name` (or equivalent) path. Folder-only strings are not reliable; to find an asset first, use `asset_index_fuzzy_search` or `asset_registry_query`, then sync the concrete path.

path.description: Asset object path to sync (aliases: object_path, asset_path). Must resolve in the asset registry—not a substitute for folder navigation.

focused.description: After success, also open the asset editor for this path when it is an object path.

failure_modes: Missing path or empty arguments: provide path/object_path/asset_path.
Path does not resolve to an asset: use discovery tools or correct the object path.

tools.summary: Cook content for a platform (UAT).
Return only the current editor selection (paths and labels); may be empty. Do not use as scene-wide discovery—use scene_fuzzy_search when the user asks to find actors in the level.

include_non_actor.description: Reserved for future non-actor UObject selection rows; currently ignored—only selected AActors are returned.

tools.summary: Read the current active editor mode.
Switch editor mode (Place, Landscape, ... allow-listed).

Replace editor selection with level actors only. Paths must be world/level actor paths (e.g. ...PersistentLevel.ActorName), not /Game/.../Asset.Asset content object paths. For Content Browser assets use content_browser_sync_asset, asset_open_editor, or asset_index_fuzzy_search to resolve paths first.
failure_modes: Passing /Game/... asset paths returns ok:false with suggested_correct_call toward content_browser_sync_asset; use scene_fuzzy_search to discover level actor paths.

tools.summary: Read a single allowlisted property from an entity (currently actor-focused). Prefer this for generic property reads when key-based access is requested.

entity_ref.description: Entity identifier. For actor, pass full world actor path.

tools.summary: Set a single allowlisted property on an entity (currently actor-focused). Prefer this for key-based property writes when exact property names are known.

entity_ref.description: Entity identifier. For actor, pass full world actor path.

tools.summary: Read one allowlisted setting from viewport/editor/project scope using scope+key.

key.description: Allowlisted key by scope (examples: viewport.view_mode, editor.mode, editor.editor_focus, /Script/Section:Key for project).

tools.summary: Write one allowlisted setting in viewport/editor/project scope using scope+key+value. Project scope (/Script/...) writes DefaultEngine.ini only for keys explicitly approved in UnrealAiProjectIniSettingsAllowlist.cpp (default: none).

key.description: Allowlisted key by scope (examples: view_mode, mode, editor_focus, auto_confirm_destructive, stream_llm_chat, or /Script/Section:Key for project).

tools.summary: Read a deterministic editor/world snapshot (JSON). Use when context may be stale or before risky edits; optional asset/viewport slices via flags.

include_assets.description: If true, include summarized asset/Content Browser context when implemented.

include_viewport.description: If true, include viewport/camera hints when implemented.

tools.summary: Read recent Message Log / log tail.
tools.ue_entry_points: FMessageLogModule or FFileHelper on project log

tools.summary: Add foliage instances in radius.
Bring a registered dock tab to front.
Import heightmap to landscape.
List referencers for a material. If not found in the registry, the path may be wrong for this project—retry discovery with asset_index_fuzzy_search before concluding the asset is absent.
failure_modes: Not found: verify material_path with asset_index_fuzzy_search; path may not exist in this project.

tools.summary: Read one scalar parameter value from a Material Instance.

Set one scalar parameter on a Material Instance (write). material_path must be a UMaterialInstanceConstant object path, not a base UMaterial. If the path is a base Material, open it in the editor (asset_open_editor), duplicate to an MI, then retry with the MI path. For read-only usage intel, use material_get_usage_summary.
failure_modes: Base Material path: use asset_open_editor on the Material, duplicate to MI, then pass the MI path.

tools.summary: Read one vector parameter value from a Material Instance.
Set a vector parameter on a Material Instance (write). material_path must resolve to a UMaterialInstanceConstant, not a base Material; duplicate or create an MI first if needed.
Execute a pre-registered editor command ID.
Open MetaSound source.
Move actors to an outliner folder.
Full package build.
Execute PCG graph in editor.
Apply impulse (PIE-oriented).
Start Play-In-Editor. Optional mode: "viewport" (default, in-process PIE) or "standalone" (new process when supported). Use pie_status to verify running state and pair with pie_stop when finished.

mode.description: viewport = in-process PIE (default). standalone = separate process when supported.

tools.summary: Return PIE / play-session state: playing_in_editor, play_session_request_queued, play_session_in_progress. Use for status verification before/after pie_start or before deciding whether pie_stop is needed; do not use to actually start or stop play.
Stop current PIE session started via pie_start. Use when playtest/run requests are complete or to cleanly end runtime checks.

Read a text file anywhere under the project directory (path relative to project root; Saved/, Intermediate/, etc. allowed). During a headed harness turn, paths may use the virtual prefix `harness_step/` to read files beside the current turn’s `run.jsonl` (harness batch output), e.g. `harness_step/UnrealAiDiscoveryNotes.txt`. `relative_path` is required (`file_path` alias). Do not call with `{}` repeatedly: pass a concrete project-relative file path or use suggested_correct_call. For engine/plugin lines, read this project’s `.uproject` manifest using the actual basename (see system context “Project workspace” when present—not a placeholder name). Large or binary files may be truncated or unsuitable—use max_bytes.

relative_path.description: Project-relative file path (preferred key). Alias: file_path.

focused.description: After success, open this file in the configured source IDE (best-effort).

tools.summary: Write a text file under the project. Default safe location: Saved/UnrealAiEditorAgent/ (no extra flag). Writes elsewhere require confirm:true and confirm_project_critical:true. Always blocked: Binaries/, Intermediate/, DerivedDataCache/. Uses atomic replace. harness_step/ unchanged for harness turns.

confirm_project_critical.description: Must be true to write outside Saved/UnrealAiEditorAgent/ (e.g. Config/, Source/, .uproject).

focused.description: After success, open the written file in the configured source IDE (best-effort).

tools.summary: Move or rename under the project. Source and destination must both be under Saved/UnrealAiEditorAgent/ unless confirm_project_critical:true. Binaries/, Intermediate/, DerivedDataCache/ blocked. confirm:true required.

from_relative_path.description: Source path relative to project root. Aliases: from_path, source_relative_path.

to_relative_path.description: Destination path relative to project root. Aliases: to_path, dest_relative_path.

replace_existing.description: If true, overwrite destination when it already exists.

confirm_project_critical.description: True if either path is outside Saved/UnrealAiEditorAgent/.

tools.summary: Compile via Engine Build.bat (Windows). Blocked in normal interactive editor unless confirm_external_rebuild:true, or editor was started with -unattended, or running under automation. Prefer closing the editor and building from a script. Returns messages and raw_log_tail.

target_name.description: UBT target name (usually <UProjectBasename>Editor). Blueprint-only projects still use this editor target; game code may be empty.

confirm_external_rebuild.description: Must be true when the editor is interactive (not -unattended / not automation) so the user explicitly accepts an external UBT process.

failure_modes: Missing Windows SDK or Build.bat
Long-running; editor may appear busy

tools.summary: Capture a UTextureRenderTarget2D to disk.
Open Level Sequence in Sequencer.
Wait for shader compile completion.

Fuzzy grep project text files under Source/, Config/, and Plugins/*/Source/ (Cursor-style): typo-tolerant; ranks paths and filenames even when the user misspells or only partially remembers a name. Returns path_matches plus optional line_matches inside files. Stops after a wall-clock budget (max_wall_ms, default 12s, max 120s) with timed_out partial results. For Content assets under /Game, use asset_index_fuzzy_search (Asset Registry index).

query.description: Approximate text; may be misspelled. Matched against relative paths, filenames, and (optionally) lines.

glob.description: Semicolon-separated extension globs, e.g. *.cpp;*.h;*.hpp;*.cs. Defaults to common C++ and config extensions.

max_hits.description: Max path_matches returned (line_matches budget scales with this).

max_files_to_scan.description: Cap files opened for line-level fuzzy matching (highest path scores first).

include_line_matches.description: If true, scan text inside top-ranked files for fuzzy line hits.

max_wall_ms.description: Wall-clock time budget in milliseconds (default 12000, clamped 500–120000). Stops early with timed_out and partial matches.

failure_modes: timed_out true: partial path/line results; tighten glob, raise max_wall_ms, or reduce max_files_to_scan.
roots_enumeration_partial true: file list was cut short while discovering roots; narrow scope or raise max_wall_ms.

tools.summary: Fuzzy-search actors across the loaded editor world/level (not Content Browser assets). For /Game assets use asset_index_fuzzy_search. By default excludes engine streaming/HLOD/WorldPartition helper actors from results (see actors_excluded_by_policy); use include_engine_internals:true only for diagnostics. Stops after max_wall_ms (default 12s) or max_actors_to_visit with timed_out/actors_truncated partial results. If query is missing or blank, the handler applies a safe default (query_coerced:true)—still pass an explicit non-empty query when possible.

query.description: Approximate text; may be misspelled. Compared against actor label, name, class, path, and tags.

max_results.description: Max actors returned (sorted by fuzzy score, best first).

include_hidden.description: If true, include actors hidden in the editor.

max_wall_ms.description: Wall-clock time budget in milliseconds (default 12000, clamped 500–120000).

max_actors_to_visit.description: Max actor iterator steps (default 500000, clamped 5000–2000000); stops with actors_truncated.

include_engine_internals.description: If true, do not apply built-in excludes for HLOD/streaming helpers (still applies exclude_class_substrings). Default false.

exclude_class_substrings.description: Optional extra class-name substrings to exclude from matches (case-insensitive).

failure_modes: Missing or empty query is coerced to a default search; prefer explicit query strings to avoid irrelevant matches.
timed_out true: partial matches; narrow query, raise max_wall_ms, or raise max_actors_to_visit.
actors_truncated true: iterator cap hit; raise max_actors_to_visit or narrow search.
High actors_excluded_by_policy: many engine-internal actors skipped; use include_engine_internals only if you truly need them.

tools.summary: Fuzzy-search the Asset Registry under path_prefix (default /Game). On vague but domain-scoped user asks (e.g. something in project assets, blueprint-ish, material-like), prefer one bounded call with a topical query plus narrow path_prefix over asking the user for a keyword first; ask only if results are empty and the user text has no usable token. If query is missing or empty, you must pass a narrow path_prefix (e.g. /Game/Blueprints). Invalid shape examples: `{}` or empty query with no path_prefix. Enumeration stops after max_assets_to_scan or max_wall_ms (default 12s, max 120s)—see truncated, timed_out, assets_visited. Prefer explicit query + path_prefix for performance. When multiple matches return, downstream tools must use object_path values from this result (or a follow-up narrowed search)—do not invent paths.

query.description: Approximate text; may be misspelled. Compared against asset name, object path, and class path. When the user is vague, guess a topical token from their message rather than asking for a keyword first. If omitted or empty, you must set a narrow path_prefix or the tool returns an error (see query_coerced when a default was applied).

search_string.description: Legacy alias for query. Prefer query; accepted for compatibility with older prompts.

filter.description: Compatibility alias for query (deprecated). Prefer query.

name_prefix.description: Compatibility alias for query (deprecated). Prefer query.

path_prefix.description: Package path prefix to scope the search (e.g. /Game/Blueprints). Required when query is omitted or empty; defaults to /Game only when query is explicit.

max_results.description: Max assets returned after ranking by fuzzy score.

max_assets_to_scan.description: Max asset registry entries visited during enumeration (stops early; see truncated and assets_visited in results).

class_name_substring.description: Optional filter: asset class path must contain this substring (e.g. Texture2D, StaticMesh, SoundBase, Material).

max_wall_ms.description: Wall-clock time budget in milliseconds (default 12000, clamped 500–120000). Stops enumeration early with timed_out and partial ranked matches.

failure_modes: Missing query without path_prefix: pass path_prefix or an explicit query.
truncated true: raise max_assets_to_scan or narrow path_prefix.
timed_out true: partial matches; narrow path_prefix, add class_name_substring, raise max_wall_ms, or raise max_assets_to_scan.

tools.summary: Append line to tool audit log file.
Dolly or zoom the editor view.
Read active level editor camera transform and FOV.
Orbit the active level editor camera around a pivot.
failure_modes: no_active_viewport
pilot_conflict

tools.summary: Pan the active editor camera.
Pilot a CameraActor or exit pilot mode.
Set editor camera location and rotation.
Schedule viewport capture after N frames.
Capture active editor viewport to PNG on disk.
failure_modes: no_viewport
readback_failed
disk_full

tools.summary: Frame specific level actors by `actor_paths` (bounds + FOV fit). Each path must be a full world actor path (typically includes `PersistentLevel` as a segment plus a concrete actor name after it). Never pass `PersistentLevel` alone or other level-instance tokens as the only path—discover actors with `scene_fuzzy_search` or `editor_get_selection` first. To frame the current selection without paths, use `viewport_frame_selection`.

actor_paths.description: Non-empty list of full world actor paths, e.g. `/Game/MyMap.MyMap:PersistentLevel.StaticMeshActor_0`. The string `PersistentLevel` by itself is not a valid entry. Obtain paths from `editor_get_selection` or `scene_fuzzy_search`.

failure_modes: Missing actor_paths: discover actors first or use viewport_frame_selection if the selection is already set.
Passing only `PersistentLevel` or `WorldSettings` as a path is rejected with a suggested_correct_call to scene_fuzzy_search.

tools.summary: Frame the current editor selection in the viewport (no actor_paths required). Use when actors are already selected; use viewport_frame_actors when you have explicit paths.
tools.ue_entry_points: Editor Focus Selected / viewport framing commands

tools.summary: Read the current editor viewport rendering mode.
Switch editor viewport rendering mode (Lit, Wireframe, Unlit, …). Does not require actor selection or viewport framing—call this tool directly when the user asks to change view mode.
Deprecated: not exposed to the model. Persist unreal_ai.todo_plan via tool call (legacy). Use Plan chat mode for unreal_ai.plan_dag + executor instead.

Create a new asset under /Game via IAssetTools (asset_class path + optional factory_class). Use for Blueprint assets/materials/sounds/etc. `{}` is invalid: `package_path`, `asset_name`, and `asset_class` are required on first attempt. Never call this tool until those fields are concrete (prefer values from context/discovery/history). Keep package_path under /Game. For Character/Pawn gameplay, create a Blueprint asset (/Script/Engine.Blueprint) then set parent_class via blueprint_apply_ir create_if_missing flow.

package_path.description: Long package path folder under /Game, e.g. /Game/AI/Generated

asset_class.description: Native asset UClass path (not content path), e.g. /Script/Engine.Blueprint, /Script/Engine.Material, /Script/LevelSequence.LevelSequence. Do not pass gameplay actor classes like /Script/Engine.Character here.

class_path.description: Compatibility alias for asset_class. Prefer asset_class.

factory_class.description: Optional UFactory class path when the engine requires a factory

focused.description: After success, open the new asset in its editor (uses object_path from the result).

failure_modes: missing_required_fields: package_path, asset_name, and asset_class are required (`class_path` is alias for asset_class).

tools.summary: Read/export editable UObject properties under /Game as JSON (reflection). Read-only inspector used to understand current values before applying a delta with asset_apply_properties.

Write/apply JSON property deltas to an asset under /Game (reflection / ImportText). Mutation tool: use after a concrete object_path is known and prefer dry_run before irreversible edits. `{}` is invalid: object_path + properties are required. Materials do not have Text/UMG fields—if you see status material_text_property_confusion, use in-world text (TextRenderActor/UMG) instead of inventing Text on Material assets.

properties.description: Map of property name to JSON value

failure_modes: status material_text_property_confusion: property keys look like Text/widget fields on a Material/MaterialInstance; follow suggested_correct_call or use level text actors.
status apply_errors: unknown or non-editable property names—use asset_export_properties first to list valid keys.

tools.summary: List Asset Registry referencers for an asset identifier. Pass object_path copied from a prior discovery tool (asset_index_fuzzy_search, asset_registry_query, etc.)—do not invent /Game paths.

object_path.description: Full asset object path (e.g. /Game/Folder/MyAsset.MyAsset). Same key as asset_open_editor—not the `path` key used by content_browser_sync_asset.

tools.summary: List Asset Registry direct dependencies for an asset.

object_path.description: Full asset object path (e.g. /Game/Folder/MyAsset.MyAsset). Same key as asset_open_editor—not the `path` key used by content_browser_sync_asset.

tools.summary: Create a Level Sequence asset under /Game (typed shortcut over asset_create).

Read-only: export Blueprint graph IR for an existing Blueprint asset path. `blueprint_path` is required and should come from prior discovery output (asset_index_fuzzy_search / asset_registry_query object_path), not a guessed name. Use before blueprint_apply_ir when patching logic.

blueprint_path.description: Blueprint asset path under /Game. Accepted forms: /Game/Folder/MyBP or /Game/Folder/MyBP.MyBP.

focused.description: After success, open this Blueprint editor (optional graph_name).
